title: Four-ball vs foursomes: don't mix them up
microFact: Four-ball: partners each play their own ball, best score counts. Foursomes: partners share ONE ball, alternating shots.
lesson:
Two classic partner formats with confusingly similar names:

FOUR-BALL (better ball): you and a partner EACH play your own ball; the LOWER of your two scores counts on each hole. Forgiving and social — your partner's par erases your 8. Most weekend team games are four-ball.

FOURSOMES (alternate shot): you and your partner share ONE ball. One drives the odd holes, the other the evens, and you alternate every shot until holed. Ruthless — your partner tees into a bush, YOU play from the bush. It's fast, old-school, and beloved at traditional clubs; also the format that wrecks friendships and Ryder Cup pairings alike.

GREENSOMES: the gentler hybrid — both drive, pick the better one, then alternate from there.

When the club noticeboard says "Saturday: 4BBB Stableford", that's Four-Ball Better-Ball with Stableford points — each plays their own ball, better net points count. Now you can read it.

quiz.question: In foursomes, your partner hits your team's drive out of bounds. Who plays the next stroke?
options: Your partner — they made the mistake
quiz.explanation: Alternation is strict (penalty strokes don't change the rotation) — the re-tee after your partner's OB drive is yours. Character-building.
